title: solanum tuberosum subsp. pearcei
intro: Solanum tuberosum subsp. pearcei, also known as the Andean potato, is a subspecies of the potato plant (Solanum tuberosum) native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The Andean potato has been consumed for its health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and providing essential nutrients such as potassium, fiber, and vitamins. Herbal preparations of Solanum tuberosum subsp. pearcei include decoctions and infusions, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions.
